methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Greenroom Robotics is a Series A-stage defense and security robotics company founded by Harry Hubbert. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 public firms operating in the Defense & Security sector.

The peer-median method, the sole quantitative triangulation approach applicable to current available data, surveyed three same-stage, same-sector comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M). The median valuation across this set is $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M. This method reflects current market pricing for Series A defense tech operators and provides the primary anchor for valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered on $300M. This range reflects the peer-median point estimate with modest adjustability for company-specific factors. Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed fundraising amounts, lack of operational metrics (revenue, customer concentration, technical differentiation), and limited visibility into the three comparable firms' financials beyond stage and headline valuation. A fuller assessment would benefit from comparable company revenue multiples, growth rates, and customer acquisition patterns within the defense robotics subsector.
